response.overview: Arsenal's 2024/25 season was a blend of European brilliance and domestic frustration, finishing second in the Premier League, exiting early in domestic cups, and reaching the Champions League semi-finals. Premier League Campaign  Arsenal finished second in the Premier League, securing 74 points from 20 wins, 14 draws, and 4 losses, scoring 69 goals and conceding only 34  Despite starting strongly with a 2–0 win over Wolverhampton, Arsenal never topped the league table and experienced a four-game winless run between October and November, which diluted their title challenge  Injuries to Bukayo Saka, Martin Ødegaard, and Gabriel Magalhães hampered momentum, and disciplinary issues—including six red cards—further disrupted rhythm
response.title: Arsenal - Season Review 2024-2025
